title: bindings: Add support for `Delete` for deleting manifest list from local storage.
body:
Bindings already support `Remove` which removes a manifest from the list
following function adds support for removing entire manifest for local
storage.

Similar functionality can be also used indirectly by using `Remove` defined in
image bindings

Closes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14735

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```
Bindings: Manifest package now exposes ManifestRemove to remove manifest list from local storage
```

@Luap99 Not sure but I think there is a issue with the way `podman-remote` is doing `manifest rm` it does not sets `LookupManifest` while making actual remove call see https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/domain/infra/tunnel/images.go#L31 also its not giving a good UX for `pkg/bindings/manifest` so adding this new API should make it more clean and once merged `manifest-rm` for remote can start using this instead of wiring `LookupManifest` across the API.

But i already mentioned that this just an easier alternative here https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14735#issuecomment-1167170012 and i'm also fine to keep using older API as is.

> @Luap99 Not sure but I think there is a issue with the way `podman-remote` is doing `manifest rm` it does not sets `LookupManifest` while making actual remove call see https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/domain/infra/tunnel/images.go#L31

If the option is ignored, we need to fix it.  I am OK with adding a new manifests/rm endpoint but the other bug should be fixed as well.
It screams for a better regression test as well.

> It screams for a better regression test as well.

@vrothberg I agree I think `podman manifest rm` should test if it resolves to manifest list instead of image when both exists before removing. Could you let me know if we need any new test in this PR or improve test for `podman-remote manifest rm` when we instrument that I think you meant the latter one but just confirming. 

I see two followup PR
* Fix `images` API and `tunnel) Remove(` so it accepts and honor `LookupManifest`
* Instrument `podman-remote manifest rm` to start using new API in bindings ( preferably after we fix existing API )

> > It screams for a better regression test as well.
> 
> @vrothberg I agree I think `podman manifest rm` should test if it resolves to manifest list instead of image when both exists before removing. Could you let me know if we need any new test in this PR or improve test for `podman-remote manifest rm` when we instrument that I think you meant the latter one but just confirming.

Whatever tests we add should fail with a previous version of Podman.  Since the LookupManifest field is ignored, we need a test that would fail if it's unset. Presumably when there's a local manifest with a matching image.  In that case, the matching image should be removed prior to the fixes (and not the manifest).

> I see two followup PR
> 
>     * Fix `images` API and `tunnel) Remove(` so it accepts and honor `LookupManifest`

Locally it should work. Remotely it needs fixing.

>     * Instrument `podman-remote manifest rm` to start using new API in bindings ( preferably after we fix existing API )

Yes, that sounds good to me.

@vrothberg Yes SGTM I'll address the test fixup in a follow PR for `podman-remote manifest rm` which will actually exercise the test. Since this is bindings PR I have already added a test for this. Let me know if anything else needs to be done on this PR.
